Mentorship Impact Report

C.A.S.T × Humanistic Foundation Inc.

8 semesters of transforming potential into purpose for at-risk youth in the STRTP program.

Fall 2022 — Spring 2026
The Partnership

Building a Bridge Between Mentorship and Residential Care

The C.A.S.T (Creating A Solution Today) Mentorship Program and Humanistic Foundation Inc. have partnered to serve youth in the Short-Term Residential Therapeutic Program (STRTP). Over the course of 8 semesters, this collaboration has brought consistent, positive mentorship directly into the lives of youth navigating the most challenging chapter of their development. Despite the high turnover rates inherent to STRTP settings, the program has maintained strong engagement and meaningful outcomes every single semester. This report highlights the measurable impact of our work together, and the value of continuing this partnership into the future.

Cumulative Impact

8 Semesters by the Numbers

Each semester brings a new cohort of youth, and each cohort receives the full depth of the C.A.S.T experience. The high turnover in STRTP settings means our mentors continuously reach new young people, expanding the program's total impact far beyond what a static group would achieve.

Clinical Integration

How Mentorship Notes Support Mental Health Staff

One of the most valuable, and often overlooked, benefits of the C.A.S.T program is the detailed session notes our mentors provide after every group meeting. These notes give Humanistic Foundation's mental health clinicians a direct window into how each youth is engaging socially, emotionally, and behaviorally outside of a clinical setting. Because youth often present differently in therapy than they do in peer group environments, these mentorship notes offer context that traditional assessments alone cannot capture.

"The session notes from C.A.S.T give us insight into how the youth interact in real social settings. It helps us tailor treatment plans with information we would not have access to otherwise."

Mental Health Staff, Humanistic Foundation Inc.
1

Real-Time Behavioral Insight

Mentorship notes capture how youth respond to peer dynamics, conflict, praise, and new experiences in a non-clinical setting, giving clinicians a fuller picture of each resident's emotional state.

2

Enhanced Treatment Planning

Clinicians can cross-reference mentorship observations with therapy sessions, identifying patterns and breakthroughs that inform more targeted and effective treatment goals.

3

Early Identification of Concerns

Mentors often notice shifts in mood, withdrawal, or emerging conflicts before they escalate, allowing the clinical team to intervene proactively rather than reactively.

4

Continuity Through Turnover

As residents transition in and out of the STRTP, mentorship notes create a documented history of engagement that supports smoother handoffs between care teams and incoming clinicians.

Moments That Matter

"C.A.S.T will not change you. C.A.S.T will give you the information and tools you need to change for yourself."

Rene Small, Founder & Program Director
Program Pillars

How C.A.S.T Creates Lasting Impact

Positive Influence Groups

Our group activities provide youth with a supportive and positive environment to develop social skills, build self-esteem, and form healthy relationships. Through engaging group activities and discussions, our experienced mentors empower youth to make positive choices and overcome challenges, fostering personal growth and resilience.

Experiential Development

We provide at-risk youth with opportunities to engage in new and enriching experiences beyond their usual comfort zones. Through carefully curated activities and challenges, our mentors guide youth to explore uncharted territories, encouraging personal growth and broadening perspectives while igniting a sense of curiosity and adventure.

Self-Discovery & Goal Setting

Self-discovery and goal setting are a big part of what we do. Helping youth explore their passions, strengths, and values while setting meaningful goals for their personal and career growth all contribute to their self-development and readiness for adulthood.

High Turnover, Higher Impact

In an STRTP environment, youth are constantly transitioning in and out of the facility. While this creates challenges for continuity, it also means the C.A.S.T mentorship program reaches far more young people than a fixed cohort model ever could. Every new resident who walks through the door receives the same quality of mentorship, engagement, and care. The result is a ripple effect that extends well beyond the walls of the facility.

Semester Completion Rate
Looking Forward

The Next Chapter Together

Over 8 semesters, C.A.S.T and Humanistic Foundation Inc. have built something rare: a mentorship pipeline that remains effective regardless of the constant changes within the residential environment. The data shows consistent engagement, expanding reach, and meaningful integration with clinical staff.

As we look ahead, the opportunity is clear. Continuing this partnership means continuing to reach every new young person who enters Humanistic Foundation's care with the mentorship, structure, and positive influence that the C.A.S.T program provides. Together, we are not just running a program. We are building a foundation for lasting change, one semester at a time.

C.A.S.T × Humanistic Foundation Inc.
